source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 1. 2차 CE 인허가/Attachment 10. Clinical Evaluation/Attachment 10-4 Post Market Surveillance Plan/Post Market Surveillance Plan (Doc.No.A-PMS-003).docx

document.title: Post Market Surveillance Plan
standards: MEDDEV 2.12/2 rev 2 Post Market Clinical Follow-Up
EN ISO 14971:2012 Application of Risk Management to Medical Devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: PMS plan for the ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector under CE MDD clinical evaluation documentation.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies of H&abyz Co., Ltd.
keyPoints: PMS is intended to gain and review post-production phase experience.
The device is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children, not for mammography.
The device is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
PMS sources include literature reviews, media searches, and incident and recall cases for similar devices.
PMCF study is stated as not required for ADD because safety and clinical performance are already known from previous use and PMS can provide sufficient data.

evidence.evidenceText: The objective of the Post Market Surveillance (PMS) is to gain and review the experience in the post production phase according to: MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC MEDDEV 2.12/2 rev 2 Post Market Clinical Follow-Up MEDDEV 2.7.1 rev.4 Clinical Evaluation EN ISO 14971:2012 Application of Risk Management to Medical Devices

evidence.claim: ADD is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children... Not to be used for mammography.
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The device is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX under MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

evidence.claim: The marketing history lists no countries where the product is marketed and no date of first market introduction.
evidence.evidenceText: Countries where Product is Marketed Date of First Introduction on to Market None None

evidence.claim: PMS activities include literature reviews, media searches, and experience with similar devices.
evidence.evidenceText: Literature Reviews * Clinical Evaluation Report The Media * Yahoo, Google Experience with Similar Devices Made by the Same of Different Manufacturer * Incident and recall cases

evidence.claim: Similar-device harmful case and recall information is to be investigated through Korea MFDS, Europe GOV.UK drug-device alerts, and FDA recall sources.

evidence.claim: The document states PMCF study is not required for ADD.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the document primarily cites CE/MDD requirements; FDA appears only as one recall-information source.
Source metadata productSignal is HAD/A/F 1417-1717, but the document text identifies the model as ADD and does not mention HAD/A/F 1417-1717.
Marketing history fields state None for both marketed countries and first introduction date.
